original_title: toctree contains reference to nonexisting document 'genindex', 'modindex', 'search'
original_body:
toctree contains reference to nonexisting document 'genindex', 'modindex', 'search'
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
A lot of users try to add the following links to the toctree:
```
* :ref:`genindex`
* :ref:`modindex`
* :ref:`search`
```
like this:
```
.. toctree::
   :maxdepth: 1
   :caption: Indices and tables

   genindex 
   modindex
   search
```

See:
* https://stackoverflow.com/questions/36235578/how-can-i-include-the-genindex-in-a-sphinx-toc
* https://stackoverflow.com/questions/25243482/how-to-add-sphinx-generated-index-to-the-sidebar-when-using-read-the-docs-theme
* https://stackoverflow.com/questions/40556423/how-can-i-link-the-generated-index-page-in-readthedocs-navigation-bar

And probably more.

However when doing this we get:
```
$ make html
...
.../index.rst:30: WARNING: toctree contains reference to nonexisting document 'genindex'
.../index.rst:30: WARNING: toctree contains reference to nonexisting document 'modindex'
.../index.rst:30: WARNING: toctree contains reference to nonexisting document 'search'
...
```

**Describe the solution you'd like**
The following directive should be possible and do not rise errors:
```
.. toctree::
   :maxdepth: 1
   :caption: Indices and tables

   genindex 
   modindex
   search
``
enhanced_title: Support genindex, modindex, and search as valid toctree entries without warnings
enhanced_body:
## Problem Description
Users frequently attempt to include standard Sphinx-generated pages (`genindex`, `modindex`, and `search`) in their `toctree` directives, but this results in warnings about nonexisting documents.

## Current Behavior
When users add the following to their `toctree`:
```rst
.. toctree::
   :maxdepth: 1
   :caption: Indices and tables

   genindex
   modindex
   search
```
They receive warnings during build:
```
.../index.rst:30: WARNING: toctree contains reference to nonexisting document 'genindex'
.../index.rst:30: WARNING: toctree contains reference to nonexisting document 'modindex'
.../index.rst:30: WARNING: toctree contains reference to nonexisting document 'search'
```

## Expected Behavior
The `toctree` directive should recognize `genindex`, `modindex`, and `search` as valid special entries without generating warnings, as these are standard Sphinx-generated pages.

## Reproduction Steps
1. Create a Sphinx project with a `toctree` containing `genindex`, `modindex`, or `search`.
2. Run `make html`.
3. Observe the warnings in the build output.

## Affected Components
- `sphinx.builders.html` (handles HTML build process)
- `sphinx.environment` (manages document references)
- `sphinx.util.nodes` (handles node processing)

## Environment Details
- Sphinx version: All versions (issue persists across releases)
- Build target: HTML (primarily affects HTML builder)

## References
- Related StackOverflow questions:
  - [How can I include the genindex in a Sphinx TOC?](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/36235578/how-can-i-include-the-genindex-in-a-sphinx-toc)
  - [How to add Sphinx generated index to the sidebar?](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/25243482/how-to-add-sphinx-generated-index-to-the-sidebar-when-using-read-the-docs-theme)
  - [How can I link the generated index page in ReadTheDocs navigation bar?](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/40556423/how-can-i-link-the-generated-index-page-in-readthedocs-navigation-bar)

## Proposed Solution
Modify the `toctree` processing logic to treat `genindex`, `modindex`, and `search` as special reserved entries that do not require explicit document files."
```
